questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A string of length $l$, tied to the top of a pole, carries a ball at its other end as shown. On giving the ball a single hand blow perpendicular to the string, it acquires an initial velocity $v_0$ in the horizontal plane and moves in a spiral of decreasing radius by curling itself around the pole. Therefore,
options: the instantaneous centre of revolution of the ball is the point of contact of the string with the pole at that instant.
the instantaneous centre of revolution of the ball will be fixed at the point where the string was initially fixed.
the angular momentum of the system will not be conserved.
the angular momentum of the system will be conserved.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Two masses move on a collision path as shown. Before the collision the object with mass 2M moves with a speed v making an angle $\theta=\sin ^{-1} \frac{3}{5}$ to the x -axis while the object with mass M moves with a speed $\frac{3}{2} v$ making an angle $\phi=\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}$ with the x -axis. After the collision the object of mass 2 M is observed to be moving to the right along the x -axis with a speed of $\frac{4}{5} v$. There are no external forces acting during the collision. The correct option is
options: The velocity of mass M , after the collision, is zero.
The centre of mass is moving along x -axis before the collision.
The velocity of centre of mass after the collision is $\frac{5}{2} v$
The total linear momentum of the system before the collision along x - axis is $\frac{5}{6} M v$

In operation, the plates move back and forth horizontally, perpendicular to the axis of roller, and the roller rolls freely between plates without slipping on either one. At that instant, for the roller
options: The liner speed of its axis is $12 \mathrm{~cm} \mathrm{sec}^{-1}$ towards right
The linear acceleration of its axis is $20 \mathrm{~cm} \mathrm{sec}^{-2}$ towards left

questions.q: A lawn roller is a solid cylinder of mass M and radius R . As shown in the figure, it is pulled at its center by a horizontal force F and rolls without slipping on a horizontal surface. Then the
options: acceleration of the cylinder is $\frac{2 F}{M}$
force of friction acting on the cylinder is $\frac{2 F}{3 M}$
